title: SessionTrustBundle
description: AITP v0.2 Session Trust Bundle (RFC-AITP-0010 §3). A coordinator-attested membership artifact for multi-agent sessions: redistributes N bilateral coordinator↔participant Mutual Handshakes to all participants without an O(N^2) full mesh. The bundle itself is JCS-profile signed; participant TCTs are embedded as opaque compact JWS strings.

session_id.description: UUID v4 unique to this session. Used as a replay-binding scope.

coordinator.description: The coordinator's AID. MUST match the `iss` claim of every embedded TCT.

issued_at.description: Unix timestamp when this bundle was signed.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp after which the bundle MUST NOT be used. MUST equal `min` of the embedded TCTs' `exp` claims (RFC-AITP-0010 §6).

participants.description: Array of participant entries. Each pairs a participant AID with the peer-issued TCT the coordinator issued during the bilateral handshake that fed into this bundle.

aid.description: Participant's AID. MUST equal the embedded TCT's `aud` claim.

tct.description: Verbatim coordinator-issued TCT as a compact JWS string (typ 'aitp-tct+jwt', RFC-AITP-0005), with `aud` = participant's AID. Decoded claims validate against `aitp-tct.schema.json`. The string is covered verbatim by the bundle's JCS signature.

extensions.description: Optional extension namespace per RFC-AITP-0001 §7 and RFC-AITP-0012. Unknown keys inside `extensions` MUST be ignored; unknown fields outside it are rejected.

signature.description: Coordinator's unpadded base64url signature over the JCS-canonical bytes of `session_bundle` (RFC 8785), optionally algorithm-tagged per RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4.3. Same convention as the RFC-AITP-0008 revocation list.
